prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like moving into an ancient house where the floorboards still creak in your mother tongue; every midnight you sneak in with a candle, prying up nails of grammar and sanding rough grain of vocabulary, hoping the original wood will accept your foreign weight without splintering. At first the rooms echo, empty and accusatory, but after months of whispering strange syllables into the rafters the beams begin to answer back, timbers shifting until the whole structure sings a new resonance that carries both accents at once. One dawn you realize the house hasn’t merely let you stay—it has quietly rewired its own skeleton around your presence, and when you step outside the sunrise speaks to you in two voices without asking permission.
